source: CIS IBM CLOUD FOUNDATIONS BENCHMARK V1.1.0
title: Minimize the number of Service IDs with admin privileges in the account

input_row.source: CIS IBM CLOUD FOUNDATIONS BENCHMARK V1.1.0
input_row.title: Minimize the number of Service IDs with admin privileges in the account
input_row.description: Comply with the principle of granting least privilege by using Access Groups to manage admin privileges and by avoiding the use of many Service IDs with Administrative Privileges.
input_row.rationale: The principle of granting least privilege mandates that a Service ID must only be given access to the resources that are required to complete their task. This task can be hard to maintain when managing large numbers of users. Instead of assigning administrative privileges to individual Service ID, create Access Groups with administrative privileges and add or remove Service IDs from these Access Groups as needed. Additionally, instead of using the Platform Administrator role for all IAM-enabled services, IBM Cloud users can grant access to specific resources and account management services to better comply with the principle of granting least privilege. Impact: Managing administrative access via Access Groups simplifies the process of adding/removing admin privileges from users in the account. Having too many Service IDs with Administrative privileges goes against the principle of granting least privileges and mean many possible holes for any security attack.
input_row.audit: Console: 1. Log in to IBM Cloud at https://cloud.ibm.com. 2. From the Menu bar, click Manage > Access(IAM) . 3. Click Service IDs and select a Service ID by clicking on the Service ID name. 4. Click on the Access Policies tab. 5. View the access policies assigned to the Service ID to verify if that Service ID has Administrator Role assigned. 6. Verify that not too many such Service IDs with Administrative privileges exist API: N/A
input_row.remediation: To remove excessive number of Users with Administrative privileges, follow the following steps. 1. Log in to IBM Cloud at https://cloud.ibm.com. 2. From the Menu bar, click Manage > Access(IAM) . 3. Click Service IDs and select a Service ID by clicking on the Service ID name. 4. Click on the Access Policies tab. 5. View the access policies assigned to the User to verify if that Service ID has Administrator Role assigned. 6. If there are many such Service IDs with Administrative privileges, select to remove the Administrative privilege or Remove that Service ID from the list of Service IDs. API: N/A Default Value: By default, there are no administrator Access Groups in the account. References: 1. https://cloud.ibm.com/docs/iam?topic=iam-userroles
input_row.impact: Managing administrative access via Access Groups simplifies the process of adding/removing admin privileges from users in the account. Having too many Service IDs with Administrative privileges goes against the principle of granting least privileges and mean many possible holes for any security attack.

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Manual

**APPROACH DECISION:**
- **Manual:** This control involves verifying the administrative privileges of Service IDs, which requires human judgment to evaluate the appropriateness of those privileges based on organizational needs and compliance with the principle of least privilege. The "audit" and "remediation" procedures provided primarily describe a manual process using the IBM Cloud Console without mentioning any specific IBM Cloud API or CLI commands that could automate this verification process. 

**DECISION CRITERIA:**
- The audit involves evaluating whether there are too many Service IDs with administrative privileges, a task that involves judgment based on organizational policies and cannot be fully automated via IBM Cloud APIs.
- The control is not about a specific technical property that can be readily queried or checked through APIs (such as encryption settings, IAM policies, etc.), but rather about reviewing access configurations that may vary by organization.
- There are no API or CLI automation pathways provided in the audit or remediation steps, leading to the conclusion that this remains a manual process.

**MANUAL STEPS:** 
1. Log in to IBM Cloud at https://cloud.ibm.com.
2. Navigate to Manage > Access (IAM).
3. Under Access (IAM), select "Service IDs".
4. Review each Service ID by selecting it and going to the "Access Policies" tab.
5. Validate that each Service ID does not possess unnecessary Administrator Roles that violate the principle of least privilege.
6. Document any Service IDs with Administrator Roles and assess whether each aligns with business needs and security policies.
7. If excessive privileges are identified, the appropriate administrative actions should be taken to adjust the access levels to be more restrictive, following company policy and security guidelines.

**SOURCE:** CIS IBM CLOUD FOUNDATIONS BENCHMARK V1.1.0
